evidence.text: 1. Management in responsive and accessible 2. Work-Life Balance 3. Leave policies which are unheard of in the majority of Indian Companies in terms of paternity and Annual Leaves Excellent in terms of Work Culture and Work life balance

The company offers great employee benefits including fitness reimbursement, internet and mobile allowances, and transportation support. Medical benefits are on par with industry standards. Work-life balance is well maintained, and there are plenty of opportunities to explore and work with new technologies. The work-from-home options are also flexible, which adds to the overall comfort of working here. Good Work-Life...
Great work-life balance Benefits Remote PTO is good Great work-life balance
Pros : Work Life Balance, Women Friendly Good Work Life Balance
great work life balance, very calm majority of the year Good work life balance, a bit boring

Benefits, benefits, and benefits. This company is always reevaluating their benefits based on what the employees are wanting including salary comparisons for your area and your position. Sabbaticals, PTO, and insurance are also great things about this company. Even outside of benefits, the people that work here are great between co-workers, managers, and principles and directors. All of the higher ups take a very ac...

The benefits are truly unmatched. Unlimited PTO, gym memberships, phone or internet bills are covered, and they allow lots of flexibility with where/when you work depending on the manager. Great company and great benefits

Benefits are at or above industry standard. 12 paid holidays, unlimited pto, company wide week off in summers. Competitive salary and commission/bonus incentives. Annual merits increases and sizable market rate adjustments. Flexible work from home and hybrid office models. Great benefits and competitive salaries
Great flexibility. Leaders who really care about their employees. Continuous improvement. Affordable healthcare, and decent benefits. Great place to work, but it's still tax
Awesome benefits like free downtown parking, gym and phone reimbursements, 401(k) match, and Life insurance. Great benefits

evidence.role: Current employee - Consultant, Sales and Use Tax
evidence.text: Work-life balance, Benefits, Transparency, Executive leadership, Innovation, Growth opportunity Ryan has allowed me to achieve career success and advancement in spite of a hectic life and in the midst of a pandemic.

I feel very blessed to work for this organization. I feel valued and respected, especially during a pandemic with countless unemployed individuals. Ryan continues to hire and has adapted quite well to working from home. I work with some amazing people and I consistently feel valued and respected. The company is well organized with clear plans for continued success and growth. I genuinely appreciate the CEO and Chair...
Lots of Learning opportunities to grow in career. Great positive vibe in the company. Leadership is employee supportive Best Place to Work with Great Learning opportunities

Ryan has experienced exponential growth, thus giving employees opportunities for advancement. The Firm maintains experienced leadership, who is driven by purpose. Ryan has an incredible ability to attract, develop and retain top talent. No other organization in the tax space is better equipped to serve it's Clients. Excellent Leadership and Career Development Opportunities

US stakeholders are always encouraging and provided learning opportunities. The US team has been great to work with, and I have felt valued and supported in my role. Amazing US Stakeholders, great learning opportunities

Overall I have been impressed with the culture at our company. It fosters a positive, inclusive, and collaborative environment that makes coming to work most days enjoyable. It's fast paced - which I love! My team and manager, in particular, are fantastic. They are not only supportive but also genuinely invested in my success and well-being. The benefits package offered by the company is solid. It has come to my aid...
As a company, Ryan does offer some remote flexible work schedules depending on location. Good amount of PTO. Managers and Directors Only Care for Themselves Causing Negative Hostile Work Environment

Fully Remote with Home Office Support | The company provides flexibility to work from home and offers a home office stipend to ensure employees have what they need to be productive. Strong Benefits Package | Medical, dental, vision, AD&D, and life insurance were comprehensive and very reasonably priced. Supportive Colleagues | Team members were generally helpful and friendly, and many were willing to answer ques...

Flexible work environment and ability to work remotely. Benefits are ok. You have a good parental leave option if you have been at the job for at least a year. They contribute to your 401k and pay for a portion of your cell phone bill. Isolating work environment and low salary

1.Exposure to U.S. real estate and property tax compliance 2.Strong training programs for new joiners 3.Supportive team environment with approachable managers 4.Opportunities to develop leadership and mentoring skills 5.Clear processes and deadlines that improve efficiency Great learning in U.S. tax compliance with growth opportunities

Flexibility, fairness, appreciation, and acknowledgment for your effort is the biggest experience your get at Ryan. In my overall work experience with Ryan, I have observed that if you give your best, you will surely be acknowledged for your efforts and hard work. Flexibility, fairness, appreciation and acknowledgment
Remote Flexibility Loyalty Trust Passion Great Work
Toxic culture Don't join here Research analyst
Decent benefits and some nice perks of the office building Lacks Integrity
Great benefits comparable salaries fair and equal treatment of all employees Compensation great Hybrid work schedule Great place to work
Can't think of any at this time. Great Place to Work
Low salary but bonuses can be great Great company but low salary
Low pay; far behind market Great Perks, low pay

Low pay for comparable positions elsewhere Potential for advancement only through education, not purely through work Not great in the YoY feedback 1-2 "company/team building events" per year, even before covid Nothing Flashy, Low Pay
Too much work, not enough support. Benefits are VERY expensive! Underpaid, Overworked

As it says on Ryan's own website "Ryan employees are given the freedom to do their best work in the way they work best. With a clear understanding of expectations and accountabilities, employees have ownership of their time and flexibility to meet the demands of their professional and personal lives". However, this is not the case at all. Maintaining a healthy work-life balance is essential for personal well-being a...

Ryan is experiencing some growing pains, which can make certain processes frustrating. However, senior leadership is aware, has plans to improve them and is investing in the improvements. Great Place to Work

Worst company ever seen in my entire career, so much micro management should work more than 12 hours to complete 8 hours target management is worst specially Compliance team deta processing No work life balance

Horrible management, no incentives or appreciation, ALWAYS told how horrible we are doing but then emails say different. The let people say offensive things that. You will not have a work/life balance nor feel appreciated
No cons about this, all good Work Life balance
Low career growth Low salaries Data processing work Good environment but not for career growth

As a company that is scaling up, sometimes you might feel overwhelmed with everything that is going on, however, this is something that can play to our advantage. The room for growth and learning is enabled by the dynamic environment that shapes this company. My 2nd Year at Ryan
Not much scope in work No growth Nepotism Growth
If you are not in the senior management circle, you are ignored for everything you contribute. No incentives if you are not management No training Overlooked Employees

Higher positions do not always value his employees. Huge salary discrepancies which should be reviewed, as it created lack of motivation Best team, good learning opportunity, but not the best in termos equility
Haven't found anything to complain about, yet. The best place to work in my entire work career
Flexible work environment doesn't always mean more days off Can be high-stress Hiring process can take a long time Great place to work
Not a single cons that can be said Eco friendly
Did not focus on teaching and improving my skills Good
Salary not up to the mark Employee friendly

Racism Discrimination Micro-aggressions Sexual Assault Bullying Micromanagement Ryan, formally known as Paradigm Tax Group is the worst company Iâve ever worked for. The executive board, management and even HR believe they are untouchable. They have bulldozed everyone in their path, regardless of the situation. Their main focus is protecting the company and they will cut you down to do so. I have been sexually ass...
Unethical management and long hours Unethical
The workload was unrealistic and unmanageable and local upper management was unprofessional. This company has no loyalty. loyalty
Every layer of leadership I had personally dealt with other than the C-Suite was more proficient at playing politics than focused on driving results in my experience. Not Great
It has cons like any other large firm - politics, inertia, fixing/focusing on things that aren't broken, etc. Great Place to Work
